mnemonics: Our Super Protocol Finds (OSPF) the shortest path using Dijkstra's algorithm, like a Link-State superhero.
keyPoints: OSPF is a link-state routing protocol that uses Dijkstra's algorithm to calculate the shortest path first.
It divides an autonomous system into areas to reduce routing traffic and improve scalability, with a backbone Area 0 connecting them all.
OSPF establishes neighbor adjacencies by exchanging Link-State Advertisements (LSAs) to build a synchronized Link-State Database (LSDB).
commonMistakes: Misconfiguring the OSPF area assignments, especially forgetting that all areas must connect to the backbone Area 0.
Forgetting that OSPF cost is calculated as the reference bandwidth divided by the interface bandwidth, leading to suboptimal path selection.
